---
title: "grocery-sim"
subtitle: "A structural microsimulation of a small neighborhood grocery store, with a fully known causal ground truth"
page-layout: full
---

## What this is

`grocery-sim` generates one or three years of a small grocery store's full
paper trail (receipts, invoices, a ledger, a tax filing) from a simulated
economy of customers, an owner, and scripted macro shocks, not from sampled
distributions. Every number has a traceable, documented cause.

- **Real ground truth.** Every figure is the output of a specific,
  documented decision or shock, gradeable against a hidden answer key.
  See [Checking ground truth](ground-truth.qmd) for how that actually
  works, and every hidden table this package ships.
- **Exact counterfactuals.** Every random draw is keyed by identity, so
  deleting one scripted event and replaying the world gives the *exact*
  causal effect of that event, not an estimate of it.
- **A generated business-case brief.** Every run also produces a client:
  an owner, a letter, an intake interview, built entirely from that run's
  own settings and results. See a real one in the
  [API reference](api-reference.qmd) or a
  [worked case](analyses/case-description.qmd).
- **Realistic, gradeable mess.** Ten paperwork-defect families (duplicate
  invoices, missing deliveries, mis-typed counts, ...) planted at low
  rates, each logged to a hidden answer key.
- **A 62-question analysis catalog**, from data cleaning through causal
  inference, prediction, prescription, and policy experiments.

## How to start

In a terminal:

```bash
pip install grocery-sim
```

In a Python script or notebook:

```python
from grocery_sim import GroceryStoreSimulation

sim = GroceryStoreSimulation()
sim.setup(dict(
    basic = dict(
        name = "My Grocery Store",
        random_seed = 42,
        year = 3,
        budget = 60_000,
    ),
    events = dict(
        competitor = "2026-06-01",
        typhoon = "2025-07-15",
    ),
    potential_investment = dict(
        bigger_store = True,
    ),
))
sim.simulate()

sim.data()           # in-memory tables (receipts, invoices, ledger, ...)
sim.db()             # the same tables behind a DuckDB connection
sim.erd()            # a Mermaid ER diagram of this run's schema
sim.describe()       # a generated business-case brief for this run
```

`sim.setup({})` (or any partial dict) works too, every key not mentioned
falls back to its default. See the [API reference](api-reference.qmd) for
every setting `sim.setup()` accepts, with a full example.

## Where to go from here

- **[Theory](theory.qmd)**: the intuition behind the construction, why the
  world is split into decisions and scripts, and why that split is what lets
  a counterfactual be exactly true instead of merely estimated.
- **[Complete DGP](complete-dgp.qmd)**: the full causal graph behind every
  generated run, drawn whole, from the scripted macro layer down to the
  paperwork an analyst actually receives.
- **[API reference](api-reference.qmd)**: every public method, with real
  signatures, parameters, and output.
- **[Analysis catalog](analysis-catalog.qmd)**: the layered set of questions
  this data is built to answer, from cleaning the paperwork through
  structural, causal, and predictive modeling.
- **[Checking ground truth](ground-truth.qmd)**: how grading actually
  works, in code, and a full reference of every hidden table this package
  ships.
- **[Exemplar analyses](analyses/index.qmd)**: worked examples on real
  generated arms, added over time.

## Links

- [PyPI package](https://pypi.org/project/grocery-sim/)
- [Source repository](https://github.com/ChinhMaiGit/grocery-sim)
- [The theoretical paper (PDF)](https://github.com/ChinhMaiGit/grocery-sim/blob/main/paper/paper.pdf)
